title: Johnson & Johnson — Lung — synthesis enriched local

frontmatter.scope: enriched (local only — includes competitor study intelligence)

html:
Lung Cancer Cross-Study Synthesis — Enriched (Local Only)

CONFIDENTIAL — Vinoth's personal use only. NOT for SharePoint. This version includes intelligence from AstraZeneca Tagrisso PET studies.

Generated: 2026-04-24 J&J sources: Rybrevant PET Q4'25, Lung OCE Q4'25, Social Listening 2025 AZ sources: Tagrisso PET Q3'25 (129K chars), Tagrisso PET Q4'25 (118K chars) Earnings: J&J Q3/Q4/Q1 + AZ Q3/Q4/FY25

The 360° View: What ZoomRx Sees on Both Sides

ZoomRx runs promotional effectiveness tracking for both Rybrevant (J&J) and Tagrisso (AZ) in EGFR mNSCLC. This is an extraordinary information advantage — Vinoth can see the competitive battlefield from both sides. Here's what the combined data reveals.

1. Cross-Study Patterns (Both Perspectives)

Pattern 1: J&J Leads SOV — and AZ Confirms It

Rybrevant PET Q4'25 (J&J side): J&J SOV 54% vs AZ 46%. J&J widening the gap, driven by increased call frequency among academic HCPs.

Tagrisso PET Q4'25 (AZ side): "Rybrevant maintained its lead in SOV despite a modest decline in its reach." AZ's own tracking validates the J&J SOV advantage.

Combined insight: This isn't a data artifact — both sides agree J&J has more field presence. But both sides also show the same paradox: SOV leadership isn't converting to prescribing dominance. J&J leads in activity; AZ leads in outcomes (branded closing, actual share).

Pattern 2: AZ's FLAURA2 OS Message Is Backfiring

Tagrisso PET Q4'25 (AZ side): "FLAURA2's Final OS message was the most effective and motivating message but was equally likely to be associated with Rybrevant as Tagrisso." Less than half of MDs recalled hearing it during an AZ interaction.

Rybrevant PET Q4'25 (J&J side): NCCN guideline message is J&J's most recalled message. OS messaging from J&J (MARIPOSA) is well-established.

Combined insight: AZ's attempt to counter MARIPOSA OS with FLAURA2 OS is creating confusion. Physicians who hear "OS data in EGFR" associate it with Rybrevant (MARIPOSA) as much as Tagrisso (FLAURA2). This is a win for J&J — they established the OS narrative first, and AZ's response is being misattributed. This is a finding Vinoth can use: J&J should keep hammering OS messaging because even AZ's OS message is being credited to them.

Pattern 3: The Multi-Stage vs Single-Indication Battle

Tagrisso PET Q4'25 (AZ side): AZ tracks Tagrisso across three indications — Adjuvant (ADAURA), Unresectable Stage III (LAURA), and Metastatic (FLAURA/FLAURA2). Their PET captures interactions across all three settings. ADAURA DFS messages and LAURA PFS messages are "highly effective."

Rybrevant PET Q4'25 (J&J side): Rybrevant tracking is exclusively metastatic (1L and 2L EGFR mNSCLC). No adjuvant or Stage III data.

Combined insight: AZ's defense strategy is the "Tagrisso ecosystem" — one drug across all disease stages. Physicians who start patients on Tagrisso in adjuvant/Stage III will likely continue with Tagrisso-based regimens in metastatic disease. This is a structural moat that field force activity alone can't overcome. Rybrevant needs the metastatic-only fight to be decisive enough to overcome the upstream Tagrisso loyalty.

Pattern 4: Tagrisso-Only Physicians Are Increasing

Tagrisso PET Q4'25 (AZ side): "More than one-third of target MDs reported Tagrisso-only interactions, a noticeable increase from Q3 — while the percentage of MDs reporting both a Tagrisso and Rybrevant interaction declined."

Rybrevant PET Q4'25 (J&J side): SOV widening, but this doesn't capture whether J&J is reaching NEW physicians or intensifying with existing ones.

Combined insight: The EGFR prescriber pool is polarizing. Some physicians are becoming Tagrisso-exclusive, some are dual-exposed. The AZ data suggests the "Tagrisso-only" camp is growing. This is concerning for J&J — SOV leadership means nothing if a growing segment of physicians isn't even hearing the Rybrevant story. J&J should focus on reach (new physicians) not just frequency (more calls to existing ones).

Pattern 5: FASPRO Changes Nothing on the AZ Side — Yet

Tagrisso PET Q4'25 (AZ side): FASPRO (Rybrevant SubQ) approved late Q4 but no measurable impact on Tagrisso prescribing intent or AZ's competitive positioning in this quarter's data.

J&J Q1 2026 earnings: FASPRO driving 18.8% sequential revenue growth, attributed to new prescribers.

Combined insight: The FASPRO impact is showing up in J&J's revenue but NOT YET in AZ's competitive tracking. This means FASPRO is either (a) pulling in physicians who weren't in AZ's target list, or (b) the effect hasn't reached AZ's measurement window yet. The Q1'26 data from both sides will be the definitive read. Track this closely — if FASPRO starts showing up as a competitive threat in AZ's tracking, it means it's working at scale.

2. Thesis Check (Enriched with AZ Data)

Thesis J&J-Only Assessment With AZ Data
1. MARIPOSA OS determines ceiling STRENGTHENED STRONGLY STRENGTHENED — AZ's own OS message (FLAURA2) is being misattributed to Rybrevant. J&J owns the OS narrative.
2. Physician inertia > efficacy STRENGTHENED CONFIRMED FROM BOTH SIDES — AZ sees Tagrisso-only physicians increasing. J&J sees SOV leadership not converting. Both sides report the same inertia.
3. Presence without depth is fixable STRENGTHENED ENRICHED — AZ data shows their mid-call messaging (ADAURA DFS, LAURA PFS) is "highly effective" — suggesting structured call flow works. J&J's fragmented mid-story IS fixable if they adopt a similar discipline.
4. ADCs will own post-TKI space UNCHANGED ENRICHED — ZoomRx also runs Dato-DXd NSCLC studies for AZ (project 44 and 46). ADC competitive intelligence is available from the AZ side.
5. Community is the volume game STRENGTHENED NUANCED — AZ tracks "Target MDs" vs "Overall MDs." Their target list includes a specific on-TL/off-TL split (81%/19%). J&J's community data is weaker by comparison.

3. Contradictions & Surprises

  1. AZ's FLAURA2 OS message is J&J's best free advertising. When AZ reps discuss OS data, physicians attribute it to Rybrevant as often as Tagrisso. This is extraordinary — AZ is spending promotional effort that benefits J&J.

  2. The "both-brand" physician segment is shrinking. AZ data shows fewer physicians hearing from both companies. This suggests the market is segmenting into Tagrisso-loyal and Rybrevant-curious camps. The persuadable middle is narrowing.

  3. AZ tracks 3 indications with one product; J&J tracks 1 indication with one product. AZ's measurement infrastructure is broader, which means their strategic intelligence about Tagrisso's ecosystem defense is richer than J&J's intelligence about the competitive threat.

  4. Keytruda and Tecentriq are the stealth SOV threat. Both the J&J and AZ PETs show IO checkpoint inhibitors eating into SOV. Neither company is losing share to the other — both are losing attention to IO.

4. Actions Specific to the Enriched View

  1. Tell J&J their OS messaging is winning even on AZ's turf. The FLAURA2 misattribution finding is a powerful message for J&J's commercial team: "Keep investing in OS messaging. Your competitor's attempt to counter your narrative is inadvertently reinforcing it."

  2. Propose a "dual-tracked" analysis. Vinoth can see both PETs. Without revealing client-specific data, ZoomRx could propose a syndicated "EGFR Competitive Landscape" study that synthesizes public-domain trends from both programs. This is a new revenue stream.

  3. Monitor the Tagrisso-only physician trend. If the Tagrisso-exclusive segment keeps growing, J&J's SOV investment is increasingly going to the wrong physicians. Push the team to measure "net new prescriber acquisition" not just SOV.

  4. Use AZ's multi-indication tracking as a model. AZ's Tagrisso PET tracks adjuvant + Stage III + metastatic in one program. J&J's Rybrevant PET only tracks metastatic. As Rybrevant expands into H&N and CRC, J&J should adopt a similar multi-indication framework. Propose it before they ask.

  5. Check if Dato-DXd NSCLC PET (AZ project 46) has insights on the post-TKI landscape. This is direct intelligence on the ADC competitive threat from AZ's own tracking. Could enrich Thesis 4.

This document is for Vinoth's personal strategic use only. It combines intelligence from J&J and AZ programs that should not be shared with either client. The SharePoint version (synthesis_latest.md) contains only J&J-sourced insights.

Back to TA Knowledge Hub | Home
